pockets.heading: Spawn 3
lines: You have to salvage a ship wreck to acquire the mission objective, so don't forget to fit a salvager.

info.Damage dealt: All. Mordu = Kinetic, Thermal. Militia = EM, Explosive, Kinetic
info.Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single pocket
lines: 3 groups, aggro from group 1 at warpin. No Respawns.

pockets.heading: Group 3, 65km:
lines: The Uprising - Strike Them Where It Hurts (2/4)
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: This mission has a total of 5 waves of respawns at close distance to warpin. First wave might already have spawned when you arrive. All waves automatically aggro on you. Waves are triggered by time, not kills.

pockets.heading: Wave 5:
lines: Damage dealt: All. Mordu = Kinetic, Thermal. Militia = EM, Explosive, Kinetic
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

In the first pocket there will be 3 groups, with 1 of them aggro at warpin. Groups are close to each other, so have that in mind if you can't handle overaggro. Second pocket is nothing special, plain and easy. Full aggro at warpin. Third pocket has fairly high dps. Full aggro at warpin.

pockets.heading: Single group, 2-10km:
lines: Damage dealt: All. Mordu = Kinetic, Thermal. Militia = EM, Explosive, Kinetic
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)
You warp directly into wave 1, immediate aggro. Wave 2 are triggered by a kill, though time delayed respawn.

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: You are told to investigate nearby structures, after approaching a structure Group 1 will spawn, and Group 2 aswell only seconds later.

pockets.heading: Blitz:
lines: When Group 1 spawns, mission will complete, so you dont have to kill any rats at all if you dont want to, just warp out.

pockets.heading: Spawn 2: (Auto-Aggro)
lines: Undue Attention - Helping the Helpful (2 of 3)

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: Killing Spawn 1 will complete the mission

pockets.heading: Spawn 1: (Auto-Aggro)
lines: Doing mission on Cormorant, a Hastarius spawned instead of Hastatus, and once it was dead - another cruiser, Misthios
Undue Attention - Closing the Gatel (3 of 3)
Despite mission objective says its only doable on frigate, mission can be completed on Cormorant

pockets.heading: Warp-In
lines: Killing the 5 frigates will trigger Spawn 1

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: Killing Federation praktor Belos triggers Spawn 1

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: Objective is to destroy the Gallente Stargate. Attacking the gate triggers additional spawns, especially when getting into armor and then structure. Spawns seems to be random, including lots of frigates and a few destroyers or cruisers. Sample spawn is provided below.

info.Damage dealt: All. Mordu = Kinetic, Thermal. Militia = EM, Explosive, Kinetic
info.Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single pocket
lines: 3 groups, aggro from Group 1 at warp-in.

pockets.heading: Group 3: (65km)
lines: The Uprising - Strike Them Where It Hurts (2/4)
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: This mission has a total of 5 waves of respawns at close distance to warp-in. First wave might already have spawned when you arrive. All waves automatically aggro on you. Waves are triggered by time (ca 3 min), not kills. Take care of your drones!

pockets.heading: Spawn 5:
lines: Damage dealt: All kinds. Mordu = Kinetic/Thermal. Militia = EM/Explosive/Kinetic
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Pocket 1:
lines: 3 Groups, aggro from one of them.

pockets.heading: Single Group: (2-10km)
lines: Damage dealt: All. Mordu = Kinetic, Thermal. Militia = EM, Explosive, Kinetic
Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: The Single Spawn is triggered by a kill, though time delayed respawn.

info.Damage dealt: Explosive/Kinetic, main boss (Hoborak Moon) spews EM torpedoes, 1147.5 base damage each
info.Web/scramble: Some elite frigates may web or scramble.

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: You land right next to pretty much all the rats. The furthest ones are about 50km away while the closest ones as close as 15km. The rats are in four groups, of which the closest sometimes aggros, but sometimes you land far enough that you can aggro one at a time. Watch your drone hitpoints, they might gain aggro from nearby rats as they have to work in close proximity. The furthest group is the hardest to tank both in this room and the whole mission.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: Aggro from five battlecruisers at warp in. There are four groups total and each aggro seperately. Easy stage.

pockets.heading: Pocket 3
lines: Aggro from one battleship, two battlecruisers and one destroyer at warp in. Firing on any of the rest aggros all.

pockets.heading: Group 3: (60km)
lines: Mission is complete once all ships are dead. Battlestation in the final room drops worthless loot, mostly ammo.

pockets.heading: Note
lines: Moon may drop stuff of the value of 2mil isk.
The BS/C are tanked well, and moon is the hardest to take down.
This mission is somewhat easy, but it may take 30 minutes for the perfect ship. But consider it an hour and half to 2 hours.
This is a perfect mission to farm
No real blitz must clear all 3 pockets

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: Group 2 may or may not aggro at warp-in. One webber in the whole space. All groups within 15-30km.

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: 3x Battlecruisers (Corpatis Seer) Agg when you first enter

pockets.heading: Group 4
lines: *Cathedral drops dairy products, consumer electronics, slaver, slaver hound

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: (Last one destroyed is Trigger for Group 3)

pockets.heading: Mineable Asteroid info
lines: You warp in between sentry guns and a EM forcefield, with a small group of cruisers and a battleship firing at you. Get past the sentries, and take them out. Then the aggroing group.
There are two more groups, but they might seem a bit mixed. Fire at one rat and get aggro from one group at a time.

If you get aggro from everything, and you are not tanked vs kin, make sure you take out the named rat, as he deals a lot of damage. Had to use two repairers to keep up first time I tried this mission.
Chief Rat: Faramon Mundan: DPS: 230.20hp/s (EM: 85hp/s, Expl: 0hp/s, Kin: 43.20hp/s, Therm: 102hp/s) + Wrath cruise missile.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Kill all rats in first two pockets. In Pocket 3 you need to kill only Corpus Oracle from group 1 (to trigger group 2) and killing every rat in group 2 will flag the mission complete.

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: No aggro. Groups. Much like the Serpentis Version

pockets.heading: Pocket 2:
lines: Aggro from everything. Last rat drops the prisioners. Paon Tay has 50% kin, 60% em, 70% exp and 80% therm shield res.
No real blitz must clear first 2 pockets to proceed into 3rd room

Vengeance4sa.title: Vengeance, Level 4

info.Recommended ship classes: Battleship, Command Ship, HAC, Strategic Cruiser

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: All groups in this pocket have proximity aggro bubbles between 10-15km.
You may get aggroed by Group 1 at warp-in as its proximity aggro triggers.
DO NOT deploy drones to attack Group 1 as it will be targetted by Group 5.
Group 3 & 4 forms an aggro pair. Both will aggro if either one is attacked. Drones can be safely deployed as long as both groups have been aggroed.
Group 2 & 5 can be independently attacked in any order without drawing pocket aggro. You may deploy drones as per normal.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: The warp gate is locked until all ships have been eliminated.
Recommended order of elimination : Group 1 & 5, Group 2 and Group 3 & 4.

pockets.heading: Mineable Asteroid
lines: Pocket 1 has a sizeable Veldspar deposits.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: Auto aggro from Group 1 at warp-in. All other groups remain neutral.
Each group can be taken out independently and drones can be deployed as per normal.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: The warp gate is locked until all ships have been eliminated.

pockets.heading: Structures
lines: Sansha Storage Facility drops NPC trade groods (dairy products, livestock, enriched uranium)

pockets.heading: Pocket 3
lines: Sentry towers proximity aggro will be triggered if your ship moves to within 50km.

pockets.heading: Single Group: (55-70km)
lines: As of 20 June 2011 This room changed, there are 3-4 spawns and triggers.  Fit hard tank.
As of Crucible, no new spawns. Tower sentries don't even aggro until you're within 50km.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: It is recommended that your ship carry at least 2-3 weapon upgrade modules (e.g. Ballistic Control System, Heat Sink, etc.) when attacking Draben Kuvakei.
Mission is flagged completed once you have eliminated ALL ships(tower sentries are not required) in Pocket 3.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: There is no quick way. Pocket 1 & 2 warp gates are locked until all ships have been eliminated. The last pocket must be cleared of ships to complete the mission.

pockets.heading: Mining
lines: You can mine 1.995.000 Veldspar, 180.000 Scordite and 150.000 in all 3 stages.

Vengeance4sa.tips:
The warp gate is locked until all ships have been eliminated.
Recommended order of elimination : Group 1 & 5, Group 2 and Group 3 & 4.

The warp gate is locked until all ships have been eliminated.

It is recommended that your ship carry at least 2-3 weapon upgrade modules (e.g. Ballistic Control System, Heat Sink, etc.) when attacking Draben Kuvakei.
Mission is flagged completed once you have eliminated ALL ships(tower sentries are not required) in Pocket 3.

info.Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, secondary thermal (Don't bring thermal for last guy)
info.Video: Ishtar, Golem, Sniper Maelstrom , Bastion Kronos, Astarte CNR

Structures drop no loot. Asteroid Factory doesn't take armor damage.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: No loot from structures (Control Tower and Tactical Outpost).

pockets.heading: Group 2: (70-100km)
lines: When you start shooting at sentries (they lightly hit for everything, mostly EM and Thermal) the whole pocket aggros.

Rachen Mysuna has a 50km orbit range and 75% chance for shield boost (1000hp each 10 seconds) so low SP BS pilots can have problems breaking his tank. Rachen's 25% chance for defender doesn't help either. If you are a Raven pilot and can't handle it from distance, warp out and warp in again, then get closer (afterburner can help) and wait until he starts shooting you. Loot can be quite good (like Arbalest Cruise + Siege, named 350mm Railgun etc.).

To be honest, I heard a few times that it isn't possible to kill Mysuna without Torps. :) Well I personally finished him from 50km using T1 Wrath Cruise Missiles, without drones. All depends on skills and equipment. If you're droning be aware that he is VERY resistant to thermal. Wasps or even Wardens are preferable to Gardes and Ogres.

Rachen Mysuna is easier to kill when webbed to slow down.  Was able to take her out with a heavy missile t1 drake once a friend put 2 webs on her and slowed the speed down to 40; took less than 12 volleys then.  Without the speed her tank dies quickly under scourge heavy missiles (xr-3200 launchers too) (10/15/11)
Guristas War Installation drops rubbish loot (like 100 Light Missiles, Small NOS, 250mm T1 Railgun and Malkuth Rocket launcher).
No real blitz must clear first 2 pockets to proceed into 3rd room

I did the mission while flying a HAMGu (Heavy Assault Missle Tengu), I directly burned towards Rachen and orbitted at 7500m he couldnt hit me and since it was the first target i did not have any problems with an insane tank. So it might be that his tank gets triggered after you kill something else first. Mind it was a t2 fitted hamgu, only high meta item was the AB. It might be that you get webbed by the frigs while burning towards him, just dispatch them. It was easier than i expected.

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: Some Industrial (Clean) ships will appear and warp out before the Industrial (Suspicious) will appear.

pockets.heading: Blitz:
lines: Blow up the industrial and the mission is complete.

pockets.heading: Standings
lines: -0.0% Combat - Aggression and -0.02% Combat - Ship Kill to Gallente.

pockets.heading: 2nd Pocket
lines: Attacking the 2nd group will aggro the 4th group.
Recommended order of attack: 1st, 4th, 2nd and 3rd group.

pockets.heading: 3rd Group (80- 95km)
lines: 2x Battleship (Xevni Jipon/ Core Rear Admiral/ Flotilla Admiral)

pockets.heading: Structure
lines: Serpentis Stronghold as high hp and only drops basic T1 Modules and random ammo (not worth the time).

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: 1st and 2nd pocket: destroy all ships to unlock the  gates.
3rd pocket: destroy the 3rd group (tagging these ships makes it easier). No other kills are needed to complete the mission.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: Xevni Jipon is weak against thermal damage but has high resistances against kinetic and is immune to EW.
Careful not to get stuck on the gate and asteroids in the 1st pocket.

info.Recommended damage dealing: Kinetic, Thermal (or EM on shield and Explosive on armor)

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: You warp in with only a Caldari control tower showing.
Then you see an Industrial (clean). You dont need to kill this one.
This is followed by a second Industrial (clean). You dont need to shoot this one either. Neither of these 2 drop a wreck or loot.
Just sit and wait for a ship named Industrial (suspicious). Kill this one and an empty wreck and a cargo can will show up.
Grab the Gallentean Viral Agent from the cargo container and your mission is finished.

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: The rats will spawn one and one at a time, with 1-2 seconds in between. The Coreli Agent spawns 5-7 seconds after the others.

pockets.heading: First Spawn: (25km) (Automatic aggro)
lines: Killing the 4x Coreli Initiate will spawn:

pockets.heading: Blitz:
lines: Killing the first Coreli Agent completed the mission.

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: Initial Spawn (arrives a few seconds after entering the area):
1x Frigate (Pithi Infiltrator enters the scene 40 km away (~60 sec. later))
2nd Spawn (after destroying 2 or 3 of the Arrogators):

pockets.heading: Group 2: (30km)
lines: These frigates stream in one after another starting not long after you warp in. They all appear within ~3km of an "Abandoned Minmatar Commercial Station" which the first group starts near.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: Each new frigate spawns as soon as the previous one is destroyed. Extremely easy mission.
If the mission is blitzed, no standing loss is incurred

VitocVector1anatam.title: Vitoc Vector, Level 1

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: Approach the "Wrecked Science Vessel" which is 33km from Warp In. It's a "Spawn Container" and You have to hack it to get the mission item "Data Chip".

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: This group is appearing when You are 3km away from the "Wrecked Science Vessel".
Vitoc Vector - Mounting a Defense (3 of 4)
Recommended damage dealing: EM or therm

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: Group appearance is triggered by time.
Acceleration Gate seems not to be locked.

pockets.heading: Group 4
lines: This Group is appearing if Group 1 is killed.

pockets.heading: Group 5
lines: This Group is appearing if Group 2 is killed.

pockets.heading: Group 6
lines: This Group is appearing if Group 5 is killed.
If this Group is killed, You get aggro from all remaining groups.
Vitoc Vector - A Terrible Thing (4 of 4)
Destroy the Amarr Breeding Facility and return the enslaved Matari to your agent.

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: All Groups aggro immediately on appearance.

pockets.heading: Group 2
lines: Gives aggro when You approach the acceleration gate at around 11km.

pockets.heading: Group 3
lines: Is appearing when Group 1 is killed. Aggro.

pockets.heading: Group 4
lines: Is appearing when Group 2 is killed.

pockets.heading: Pocket 3
lines: To accomplish this mission destroy one or more of the 4 Ammarian Breeding Facilities until one of them drops Breeder Slaves. Bring them to the agent.

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: 4x Frigates named "Guard Drones" (2x Scorpionfly Apis, 1x Dragonfly Apis, 1x Incubus Apis)

pockets.heading: Tip:
lines: * Warning: Limit your enthusiasm. Trying to rescue more than the requested number of slaves may result in a HUGE fine.

VitocVector1anatam.tips: * Warning: Limit your enthusiasm. Trying to rescue more than the requested number of slaves may result in a HUGE fine.

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: Killing the last 3 Thukker Mercenary Rookie will spawn one Thukker Mercenary Fighter per Rookie ship killed from Group 2

pockets.heading: Standing Loss
lines: -4.0% with Thukker Tribe for Combat - Ship Kill

info.Space type: Type not confirmed, done with afterburner fit, with gate

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: After warping you will end up in the Broken Blue Crystal Asteroid.

pockets.heading: Tip:
lines: Exit from the Broken Blue Crystal Asteroid immediately and kill C-2 web frigates first.

WhatComesAroundGoesAround3.title: What Comes Around, Goes Around, Level 3

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: 1x Cruiser Bounty Hunter (Jason/Ikaruz/Obunga/Okochyn) (EM/Thermal + Thunderbolt Heavy Missile) web/scram

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: Remember to destroy the lookout post.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: No Blitz. You have to destroy all ships and the lookout to complete this missions.
Recommended ships: Sleipnir, Golem, CNR, Raven, Domi

pockets.heading: Pocket 1
lines: You have to kill all hostiles to unlock the acceleration gate.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: 6x Frigates (Amarr Navy Crucifier/Amarr Navy Scout/Amarr Navy Soldiers)
1x Battleship (General 'Buck' Turgidson/Apocalypse) Orbit distance: 40km (Use EM weapons!!!)
One of the Apocs might move away to approx 250km away from you, but it will return back to it's original position.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: No Blitz. You have to destroy all ships and the General's Quarters to complete this missions.

WhispersIntheDark2.title: Whispers in the Dark, level 2 Important

pockets.heading: Structure
lines: The Breeding Facility drops 2 types of random trade goods.

pockets.heading: Mineable Asteroids
lines: Veldspar - 19 x 90k asteroids - 1,710,000 units of Veldspar

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: The mission completes when you kill the Group 2. The one that is far from you.

pockets.heading: Group 2 @ 65km
lines: Last kill will drop 1 unit of "Crates of Construction Blocks" needed to bring back to complete the mission.

pockets.heading: Spawn 1 @ 60km
lines: Triggered when first group 1 ship is into hull

pockets.heading: Spawn 2 (Auto Aggro)
lines: Triggered when first Construction Freight is into hull

pockets.heading: Structure
lines: Some Sansha Bunker may drop ammo types

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Kill the two construction freighters, and pick up the crate of blocks dropped from the second (not the stack of 10 "Construction Materials").

pockets.heading: Pocket 1 (Must destroy all NPCs to unlock gate)
Group 2
lines: Aggro when you attack the Worker's Quarter

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: The mission completes when the Worker's Quarters is destroyed.

pockets.heading: Structures
lines: The Worker's Quarters will drop 10 units of 2-4 different freight goods. Also the second (starting from the landing pad) Storage Facility of the central of the 3 plants will drop goods.

pockets.heading: Warp-In
lines: Ships at 20-75k unless you choose to warp to within a different distance

pockets.heading: Note
lines: Blowing up a Stasis Tower replaces it with an Amarr Heavy Neut Tower

pockets.heading: Pocket 2
lines: This is what you see at warp in
5x Battlecruisers (Imperial Templar Justicar/Divine Imperial Justicar/Divine Imperial Champion)
There is an additional spawn in this room.  I didn't see exactly what I shot to trigger it but I was shooting the BCs first.  As there is only one Divine Imperial Champion I assume it was that one.

pockets.heading: Pocket 3
lines: Shooting the neutralizers replaces them with Sentry III towers

pockets.heading: Pocket 4
lines: I didn't get a screenshot of this pocket because I was thoroughly bored of the mission.  The objective (Datacenters) are 40k from the warp in.
I remember the Battlecruisers spawned 3 additional Battleships each.  I didn't shoot the Neut towers but I would assume they also get replaced by sentry towers.

pockets.heading: Hacking
lines: There are 4 containers each in the 2nd and 3rd room and 5 in the last room. One among each of these groups has 1 of the 3 large containers you need to finish the mission. The rest only ever contain 1 unit of scrap metal. They are bunched together at 40km from the warp in in each of the rooms. Hacking 4 was enough for me to hack all of them. To find which of the cans you need to hack make sure you have 'type' column active and the objective can should have a different type than the rest.
Hacking can in 2nd/3rd room will cause spawns to appear and attack
3rd room: ~5 elite frigs ~6 elite cruisers, 1 spawn
Hacking cans in any of the rooms will randomly cause a 500 aoe damage around the cans that hits your ship or any drones you have orbiting you.

pockets.heading: Notes
lines: The mission requires you to carry 6000m3 which will require an Industrial/Transport ship to carry.
You need to equip an Analyser module to hack the datacenters.
You need more than Hacking level 3.  I have this skill level and wasn't able to hack the datacenters.
I only took this mission because there wasn't a write up for it and both my agents gave me this mission.
If you get offered this mission you should absolutely REJECT it immediately.  It is an absolute PITA.

I apologise that the 4th room information isn't particularly accurate.  I had been running the mission for about 2 1/2 hours when I got there and once I found that you need more hacking skill than I had I just quit the mission.
There are destructible structures in most of the rooms, none of them drop anything.

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: No NPC when you'll warp-in. There will be a Caldari Consulate. (Large Collidable Structure)
Every group will pop real close to you ( < 5km ) and will aggro you.

pockets.heading: Group 1
lines: Aggro on Warp in. All following groups are appearing on site when the earlier group is completely killed.

pockets.heading: Group 4
lines: Part 2 of 5, Smile When You Call Me That

pockets.heading: Single Pocket
lines: The objective here is to destroy a Gallente Broadcast Center...
The structure itself is tough, but if you destroy the Fuel Depot just under, you'll destroy both.
In the second one, there will be a single Federation Praktor Hexeris, all you have to do is to get near it, and the mission is accomplish. (approx 4.000 meters)
You don't have to shoot it, and it won't shoot you.

pockets.heading: Second Pocket
lines: This pocket is full of Gallente ships, but You get no aggro. Once you got in range of ~47km to the Federation Navy Station, the mission is accomplished, you don't have to destroy them all.
If you stay too long, a group of 6 frigates will pop on you (Hastile and Clavis).
6 x Cruiser (Federation Praktor Ouragos and Optioon)
7 x Frigates (Federation Insidiator and Federation Praktor Balra)

info.Recommended damage dealing: EM/Therm for the Sansha. Exp/Kin for Angel.

pockets.heading: Warp-In Pocket (Warzone)
lines: First two accelerator gates are not locked and all NPCs at first and second level are optional kills, that will respawn. Only frigate and destroyer hulls may enter the first gate. Bookmarked location has Gate to Angel Base and Gate to Sansha's Base about 45km away.

pockets.heading: Group guarding Angel Base Gate: (130km) (Automatic aggro)
Angel Base Pocket
lines: Nearest group will aggro upon warp-in:

pockets.heading: Wave 2 (40km from gate)
lines: Spawns once Wave 1 destroyed and you approach the gate.
Hint:  If you wait after killing Wave 2, Wave 1 will respawn up to two more times.

pockets.heading: Wave 2 (50km from gate)
lines: Spawns once Wave 1 destroyed and you approach gate.

pockets.heading: Single Group (Aggro)
lines: The Damaged Heron will contain the required 20 Ship's Crew this time, with no respawn of any NPCs or additional Ship's Crew throughout. 20 Ship's Crew is required to be brought back to agent to complete the mission.

pockets.heading: Warp-in
lines: Auto-aggro from all after a few seconds.
If you need more range make a bookmark and warp out. Then warp to your bookmark at your optimal range.
Respawn is triggered by killing the last drone from the initial spawn.

pockets.heading: Middle Group (24-25 km from warp-in)
lines: 8x Reprogrammed Mining Drone (5 Elite Cruisers, 3 Battlecruisers)

pockets.heading: Left Group (29-30 km from warp-in)
lines: Aggroing the left or right group first will also aggro the middle group, but not the other side.  So if you shoot at the left group first, the right one will not aggro, but shoot at the right one first and the left will not aggro.  Shooting the middle group
agro both the left and right groups as well.
Drones do not pull aggro from left side.
Mission is marked complete once you land in the second pocket.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Gate is locked at warp in - Destroy gate groups
Warp to second pocket, wait for the Mining Foreman to say he's given up, warp out.

pockets.heading: Mining
lines: In Pocket 1 are 5 Roids of Veldspar 55466 Units

pockets.heading: Warp-In Pocket (Warzone)
lines: First two accelerator gates are not locked and all NPCs at first and second level are optional kills, that will respawn. Only small ships may enter the first gate. Bookmarked location has Gate to Angel Base and Gate to Blood Base about 40km away.

pockets.heading: Group guarding Blood Base Gate: (110km) (Automatic aggro)
Angel Base Pocket
lines: Nearest group will aggro upon warp-in:
There are 4 other groups further away consisting of:
The gateway to research outpost is 15km from this warp-in point.

pockets.heading: Blood Base Pocket
lines: Nearest group will aggro upon warp-in:
There are two other groups, one group consisting of:
and the other group consisting of:
The gateway to research outpost is also 15km from this warp-in point.

pockets.heading: Research Outpost Pocket
lines: The nearest group will aggro upon warp-in:
The mission will complete when this group is destroyed.

The Damaged Heron will contain the required 20 Ship's Crew this time, with no respawn of any NPCs or additional Ship's Crew throughout. 20 Ship's Crew is required to be brought back to agent to complete the mission.

pockets.heading: Group 1: (120km) (Automatic aggro)
lines: This group is guarding the Gate to the Guristas Base

pockets.heading: Group 2: (120km)
lines: This group is guarding the Gate to the Cartel Base

pockets.heading: Research Outpost Pocket
lines: Mission completes after killing the Pithi rats, but without killing the Federation rats, so you could loot the Damaged Heron and warp out without killing them.

WorldsCollide4bran.title: Worlds Collide, level 4

info.Faction: Blood Raiders and Angel Cartel (Blood Raiders in the last pocket)
info.Damage dealt: Em/Therm (very little Kin) from Bloods; All kinds, but heavy EXP/Kin from Angels
info.Recommended damage dealing: EM, Therm for the Bloods/ Exp, (Kin 2nd choice) for the Angels.

pockets.heading: Warp in: "Warzone"
lines: You arrive in the middle of two large groups (135km to the nearest rat) and two gates (40km to each gate approx). One for your primary enemy, and one for the faction mentioned in the mission briefing (in this case, Blood Raiders and Angels). They start far away from you behind their own gate. The gates are locked until you kill the last ship of that faction.  But, you can unlock them by carrying Zbikoki's Hacker Card (obtained from Zor) in your cargohold. It will be consumed on activation.

pockets.heading: Group 1 (Blood Raiders)
lines: 3x Elite Frigate (Elder Corpii Follower / Elder Corpii Herald / Elder Corpii Worshiper / Dark Corpii Upholder)
2x Elite Cruiser (Elder Corpum Dark Priest / Elder Corpum Shadow Sage)

pockets.heading: Group 2 (Angel Cartel)
lines: 3x Elite Frigate (Arch Gistii Thug / Arch Gistii Outlaw)
3x Elite Cruiser (Gistum Domination Crusher / Gistum Domination Smasher)

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: If you can't build a strong enough omni-tank based on the information provided, I'd suggest tanking EM/TH first and take the mission to completion, and then come back with your Angel Cartel tank before turning the mission in.

Both Groups are "anchored" to within about 30km of their start locations, which are about 85 to 95 km behind their gate.  This makes it easy to snipe them from the far edge of the gate if you can lock and hit from that far out.  Neither group can hit you from 55km.

pockets.heading: Blood Raider Pocket
lines: Blood Spys might scramble/web, but may not also.  Using drones on them aggros the whole room.

pockets.heading: Group 4: 7km
lines: Random chance of a Blood Overseer [375k Battleship] spawning.  It can be attacked separately without agroing other groups.  Every ship that enters the pocket gives the Overseer another chance to spawn.
Warning:  if you do it all wrong and aggro all of them --
* Full-aggro is 1100-1200dps (EM 50%/TH 50%) with the occasional kinetic cruise missile from the BS's.

pockets.heading: Tips:
lines: Shooting the Spies with your own guns/missiles will not aggro anything else. Drones and anyone else will.
Drones can be safely used to kill the auto-aggro group. Just make sure they don't shoot Spies.
One can slowboat to the next gate. No additional aggro. You will be at around 5 km to the gate when the gate group aggros you.

Wait for the 1st group to target you, and take them all out.  Fire on another ship, it's group will target you, and take that group out.  Feel free to take out the Blood Spy's, once the 2nd group is gone, to trigger the last group.
you might find a collidable structure in your way (or even hit it) as you enter.

pockets.heading: Angel Cartel Pocket
lines: Aggro at warp in: Yes (might web too)
Four apparent groups as of April 2011.

pockets.heading: Tips:
lines: Using drones on the Spies causes the whole room to aggro.
The Saints throw Bane torpedoes and can hit at 55km or more.
Arch Gistum Centurion is fast and will close the range to under 20km, so kill before the Saints.
Cherubim throw Havoc cruise missiles to at least 80km.  Thrones are shorter range.

Chance of spawning a Gist Overlord [543k battleship] as well.  Chance increases with every ship that enters the room.  Overlord waits to be attacked, and attacking it caused the remainder of the room to aggro.
Untested:  A second ship (gangmate) present may cause entire room to aggro immediately.
Original player leaving to get more ammo and returning does not cause remaining groups to aggro.

pockets.heading: 3rd Pocket (Blood Raiders)
lines: *VIP:  Killing all the ships in the 3rd pocket trips the "mission completed" flag even IF you do NOT pick up the stranded crew.
{Ie:  If you kill them all, and fail to pick up the crew, you will have to quit the mission with loss of standings from agent, corp, and faction.}
The pocket looks like a single group, but it's actually 3.  Use the same technique suggested for the Blood Raider pocket.

pockets.heading: Group 1: 5km
lines: 3x Elite Battlecruisers (Elder Corpum Dark Priest / Elder Corpum Shadow Sage)
2x Battleships (Corpus Apostle / Corpus Oracle / Coprus Prophet)
They won't move very fast, so you can get some range on them if you can find a direction to go that doesn't come close to other groups.

pockets.heading: Group 2: 10km
lines: 4x Elite Frigates (Elder Corpii Herald / Dark Corpii Upholder)

pockets.heading: Group 3: 10km
lines: 2x Battleship (Coprus Apostle / Coprus Oracle / Coprus Prophet)
Again, if you do everything wrong [use drones, etc.]
* Full-aggro is 900-950dps (EM 51%/TH 49%) with the occasional kinetic cruise missile from the BS's.

pockets.heading: Tip:
lines: Only need to blow up the auto-aggro group to get the ship crew.
Keep drones on Passive to prevent any unwanted aggro

On occasion, you might - even without drones or partners - aggro all of the groups (some of which can warp scramble). I suggest you put some distance fast between u and the groups you haven't yet aggroed.

Shooting the Spies with your own guns/missiles will not aggro anything else. Drones and anyone else will.
Drones can be safely used to kill the auto-aggro group. Just make sure they don't shoot Spies.
One can slowboat to the next gate. No additional aggro. You will be at around 5 km to the gate when the gate group aggros you.
Wait for the 1st group to target you, and take them all out.  Fire on another ship, it's group will target you, and take that group out.  Feel free to take out the Blood Spy's, once the 2nd group is gone, to trigger the last group.

The Saints throw Bane torpedoes and can hit at 55km or more.
Arch Gistum Centurion is fast and will close the range to under 20km, so kill before the Saints.
Cherubim throw Havoc cruise missiles to at least 80km.  Thrones are shorter range.

Untested:  A second ship (gangmate) present may cause entire room to aggro immediately.

Keep drones on Passive to prevent any unwanted aggro

WorldsCollide4ansa.title: Worlds Collide, Level 4

info.Damage dealt: All damage types, see notes - Mainly Exp, Kin (Angel) / EM, Therm (Sansha)
info.Web/Scramble: All Elite Frigate types. Spy frigates only webs.
info.Recommended damage dealing: Exp, Kin (Angel) / EM, Therm (Sansha); Max orbit 49km (Centus Dark Lord)

pockets.heading: Pocket 1 (War Zone)
lines: The two gates are locked until you kill the trigger ship from each faction. The trigger ships are ALWAYS a
NO aggro from the initial groups until you attack them. Each group can be aggroed independently.
Drones can be safely deployed to attack any group with aggroing the entire pocket.

pockets.heading: Angel Group
lines: NOTE: I notice since the AI update the ships react to the type of drones you use. While using long range sentry drones the foes try to get out of the drones ranges by getting closer. While using short range sentry drones the foes try to move away.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: The NPC ships will not travel more than 30km from their respective spawn points.
Drones/fleet members maybe targetted as the NPC ships reset their targetting when moving back to their spawn points.

Move to a sniping position near the respective gate, roughly 75-80km (recommended distance) from the Angel Asteroid Outpost or Sansha Watchtower. They will travel back and forth in a merry-go-round while you snipe.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2 (Angel Base)
lines: On warp-in, Group 1 will auto aggro. The Angel Spy frigates do not scramble but they do web.
DO watch for Thugs and Outlaws srcams.
All ships in this pocket have to be destroyed to unlock the gate.

pockets.heading: Warning
lines: DO NOT deploy your drones to attack Group 1. (I'm going to say this is optional. I didn't move and I had my drones attack group 1 with no problem. However, everyone in the room did target my drones and not me. T1 hobgoblin)
Any attack on the Angel Spy frigates by fleet members will aggro the entire pocket. It is safe however, for them to kill the sentry guns in Group 1.
The FIRST ship that had warped into the pocket may kill the Angel Spy frigates without repercussions, as long as you stick to turret weapons/missiles.
NO aggro from the other groups until you attack them. Each group can be aggroed independently.

pockets.heading: Group 1 (Auto-aggro):
lines: 3x Angel Sentry Guns (Tower Sentry Angel III)

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: Once Group 1 has been eliminated, it is fairly safe to deploy your drones for the remaining groups. Keep a close watch on your drones’ target to ensure that they do not stray and mistakenly attack a neutral group.
Recommended order for elimination : Group 1, Group 2, Group 3 and finally Group 4.

An Angel Overseer battleship will have a random chance to spawn in this pocket. The chance to spawn seems to be based on triggering the Angel Base warp gate in Pocket 1 and then completing the warp into this pocket.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2 (Sansha Base)
lines: On warp-in, Group 1 will auto aggro. The Sansha Spy frigates do not scramble but they do web.

pockets.heading: Warning
lines: DO NOT deploy your drones to attack Group 1.
Any attack on the Sansha Spy frigates by fleet members will aggro the entire pocket. It is safe however, for them to kill the battleships in Group 1.
The FIRST ship that had warped into the pocket may kill the Sansha Spy frigates without repercussions, as long as you stick to turret weapons/missiles.
NO aggro from the other groups until you attack them. Each group can be aggroed independently.

pockets.heading: Group 1 (Auto-aggro):
lines: 3x Battleships (Centus Dread Lord/Savage Lord/Dark Lord/Beast Lord)

pockets.heading: Group 3:
lines: 3x Battleships (Centus Savage Lord/Plague Lord/Mutant Lord/Slave Lord)

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: Once Group 1 has been eliminated, it is fairly safe to deploy your drones for the remaining groups. Keep a close watch on your drones’ target to ensure that they do not stray and mistakenly attack a neutral group.
Recommended order for elimination : Group 1, Group 2 and finally Group 3.

An Sansha's Overseer battleship will have a random chance to spawn in this pocket. The chance to spawn seems to be based on triggering the Sansha Base warp gate in Pocket 1 and then completing the warp into this pocket.

pockets.heading: Pocket 3 (Research Outpost)
lines: You will warp-in randomly around 5km from the Heron. In addition to the immediate aggro from Group 1, there is a small chance that one or more other groups will aggro due to proximity triggers.
If this happens, start moving away from the remaining neutral groups and check if the webbing/scrambling Elite Frigates have you locked. Kill them first if they have you locked.
If you are fleeted with friends, have them
focus fire on Group 1 or any groups that have been aggroed by mistake.

The FIRST ship needs to act as an Fleet Commander and assign the appropiate targets. A common mistake is to have everyone shoot random or their closest target, which leads to the whole pocket being aggroed.

Sometimes fleet members that enter later will land differently and aggro groups that were unaggroed.  If this includes the elite frigates and they scramble the new arrival, it can mean a quick death.  All fleet members should have at least enough tank to survive until the scramblers are cleared.  Having the lead ship tank the entire pocket is extremely difficult, as it can put down 1300-1500 dps, in a wide variety of configurations.  Here are some (extreme) examples:
NO drone aggro for the entire pocket. You can deploy drones normally.

pockets.heading: Tip
lines: Warping-in from either Angel or Sansha Base does not change the ship combination for this pocket. It will always be Angel ships.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Pocket 1 : Unlock the Angel Base gate either using a Zbikoki's Hacker Card (or killing the Gist War General). The card will be consumed upon activation.
Pocket 2 (Angel Base): All ships in Pocket 2 have to be destroyed to unlock the gate.
Pocket 3 (Research Outpost): Destroy Group 1, grab the hostages from the heron, fly back to your agent. No need to destroy entire groups. - confirmed in July 16, 2014.
The Zbikoki's Hacker Card can be found in Zor's wreck in “RightHandofZazzmatazz4 The Right Hand of Zazzmatazz” or "DamselInDistress4 Damsel In Distress" Level 4 mission.

pockets.heading: Video
lines: This video is one of many ways to do this mission. (The game got super glitch during the video. (I gave the GM a heads up) Anyways, I'm going to redo this mission down the road)I did leave out the fact you need to pick up the guys and bring them back to your agent.

WorldsCollide4ansa.tips:
The NPC ships will not travel more than 30km from their respective spawn points.
Drones/fleet members maybe targetted as the NPC ships reset their targetting when moving back to their spawn points.
Move to a sniping position near the respective gate, roughly 75-80km (recommended distance) from the Angel Asteroid Outpost or Sansha Watchtower. They will travel back and forth in a merry-go-round while you snipe.


Once Group 1 has been eliminated, it is fairly safe to deploy your drones for the remaining groups. Keep a close watch on your drones’ target to ensure that they do not stray and mistakenly attack a neutral group.
Recommended order for elimination : Group 1, Group 2, Group 3 and finally Group 4.
An Angel Overseer battleship will have a random chance to spawn in this pocket. The chance to spawn seems to be based on triggering the Angel Base warp gate in Pocket 1 and then completing the warp into this pocket.


Once Group 1 has been eliminated, it is fairly safe to deploy your drones for the remaining groups. Keep a close watch on your drones’ target to ensure that they do not stray and mistakenly attack a neutral group.
Recommended order for elimination : Group 1, Group 2 and finally Group 3.
An Sansha's Overseer battleship will have a random chance to spawn in this pocket. The chance to spawn seems to be based on triggering the Sansha Base warp gate in Pocket 1 and then completing the warp into this pocket.


Warping-in from either Angel or Sansha Base does not change the ship combination for this pocket. It will always be Angel ships.

info.Damage dealt: Ki/Th from Gallente, EM/Th from Sansha
info.Web/Scramble: Sansha's Berserker / Centii Loyal Ravener / Centii Loyal Scavenger
info.Recommended damage dealing: Therm vs. Gallente & Minmatar, EM vs. Sansha

pockets.heading: Pocket 1 (War Zone)
lines: Each gate is locked until you kill a trigger from each group.  Gates can, however, be activated, before killing the trigger, by using a Zbikoki's Hacker Card.
Groups aggro individually and will not fly further than ~30km from their control towers.

pockets.heading: Gallente Group
lines: 3x Elite Frigate (Federation Navy Gamma I Support Frigate)

Strongly recommend using Thermal missiles against the Federation Navy Orion, even if you're flying a Drake or Tengu, as they'll deal 2x the damage, even after the 25% damage bonus from the hull / offensive subsystem is taken into account.

pockets.heading: Pocket 2 (Federation Base) (Auto-aggro)
lines: 3x Elite Frigate (Federation Navy Delta II Support Frigate)

pockets.heading: Group 1 (Auto-aggro):
lines: 3x Battleships (Centus Dread Lord/Savage Lord/Dark Lord/Beast Lord)

pockets.heading: Group 3:
lines: 3x Battleships (Centus Savage Lord/Plague Lord/Mutant Lord/Slave Lord)

pockets.heading: Random Spawn (Auto-aggro):
lines: note: if anything (except the frigates target) engage web frigates, the hole pocket attack that target and then you. So the engaged target have to kill web frigates with their guns/missiles.

pockets.heading: Pocket 3 (Pirate Stash)
lines: Groups aggro when engaged or on proximity.  Initial auto-aggro will vary, depending on which group you land closest to.

pockets.heading: Blitz:
lines: Pocket 1: Approach the Sansha gate kill the triggers or use Zbikoki's Hacker Card to jump in. (the card is consumed in process)

Pocket 2: Kill web frigates with your guns/missiles then approach the acceleration gate (be sure that you don't go near group 2 or group 3 in order to do that go to smaller end of acceleration gate) and use it.
Pocket 3: Kill all Ships. (sometimes the cargo is unlocked before you kill all ships)

info.Faction: Serpentis & Guristas Pirates. Serpentis in the last pocket.

pockets.heading: The Warzone (Warp in area)
lines: You arrive at distance from two large groups (Serpentis and Guristas) guarding their respective gates. The gates are locked until you kill the last ship (see blitz).
The Guristas (Pith) ships will aggro your drones after a while.
The ships are sitting at a ~100km away from their gates, best to have some long range sniper ship or a fast one.

pockets.heading: Guristas Group (122- 125km)
lines: 2x Cruiser (Dire Pithum Eraser/ Dire Pithum Abolisher)

pockets.heading: Rare Spawn (25km)
lines: Gate to Serpentis Base is at 29km.
Gate to Guristas Base is at 26km.

pockets.heading: Serpentis Base
lines: Aggro at warp-in from one group. 4 Groups total.
All pretty close and can apply a lot of DPS if full room aggro occurs (which happens sometimes on warp-in).
Guardian Veterans may web and scramble. Battleships in all groups. And there's two Angel spies in the pocket.

pockets.heading: Group 1 (7km, Beacon)
lines: 2x Battleship (Core Rear Admiral/ Core Flotilla Admiral)

pockets.heading: Group 4 (25- 30km)
lines: 3x Battleship (Core Rear Admiral/ Core Port Admiral)

pockets.heading: Group 5 (25km)
lines: Gate to the Research Outpost is at 21km.

pockets.heading: Structure
lines: The Serprentis Stonghold has a lot of HP and drops assorted ammo and modules (not worth the time).

pockets.heading: Group 2 (20- 30km, Broken Orange Crystal Asteroid)
lines: 2x Battleship (Pith Eliminator/ Exterminator/ Massacrer/ Ursuper/ Extinguisher)

pockets.heading: Group 3 (20- 30km, Open Space)
lines: Gate to the Research Outpost is at 21km.

Distribution within the 3 groups not yet perfect. Haven't had time to sit back, relax and write it all down perfectly. Will do shortly.
Aggro from one. For me when I did shoot group 3, group 2 did aggro as well. A single Sansha's Demon is also spotted.
The ships will aggro other Fleet Members when they arrive.

pockets.heading: Research Outpost (Common 2nd Pocket with Damaged Heron)
lines: Warp in beacon is in the middle of 4 groups. Great for short range weapons. No web/ scramblers. Aggro from one group.
If you shoot only the aggro Group, you won't get stage aggro. After finishing the first group take an NPC near the gate and you will get aggro from the next group and so on.
There's a damaged Heron in the middle as a spawn container. Open it, and loot 20 "Ship's Crew" when you have destroyed close to everything.

pockets.heading: Group 2 (5- 10km)
lines: 3x Battleship (Core Rear Admiral/ Vice Admiral/ Flotilla Admiral)

pockets.heading: Group 4 (5- 10km)
lines: Gate to the Warzone leads back to the warp in area, the Warzone Stage.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Warp through the Guristas Acceleration Gate using Zbikoki's Hacker Card (obtained from Zor in the 'Right Hand of Zazzmatazz' mission). It will be consumed on activation.
Kill the 4 battleships to unlock gate. Kill Group 1 (spawns first) to "unlock" the Heron. It consists of 3 Battleships and 3 Battlecruisers.
When the last ship is destroyed, you can loot the crew from the Heron and leave.
None of the other groups that spawn soon afterwards aggro, and neither do they object to drones.

pockets.heading: Optional Approach
lines: Sniper [Sentry] fit with MJD. Snipe from warp in beacon in first pocket, best to Micro Jump right away to snipe in all other pockets unless heavily tanked.

pockets.heading: The Overseer
lines: The Overseer is a special ship [Dominix] that rarely spawn in the 1st room. It has, in its description: "Sometimes they hire a good pilot to fly their ships" and has a value between 487K and 4.8 mil. The higher value one has a MWD (moves at ~600m/s), but has low resists and doesn't aggro until you shoot it. It drops faction loot with a low possibility of a Serpentis Blueprint. It's spawn point is with the group that does not aggro at the beginning of the stage.

info.Faction: Gurista Pirates & Serpentis (Gurista Pirates present in the last pocket)
info.Space type: Deadspace, with guards at the first gates.

pockets.heading: Warp in "Warzone stage"
lines: You can blitz this by using Zbikoki's Hacker Card. (Obtained from Zor in other missions). It will be consumed upon unlocking the gate.

pockets.heading: Guristas group
lines: Drones steal aggro sometimes in Serpentis group. Probably due to range. That never happened to me for the Gurista group though.
non-ganged logistical ships seem to not get aggro. Cloak outside of the clouds between the 2 groups to be safe.

Tip: if you go from the clouds of Guristas group to the Serpentis group and stop 74k ish from the gate of the Serpentis group only the BS will shoot you. all the rest will be out of range and never get close enough due to some kinda mission setting to stop them going too far from the gate.
: Both groups have a bunker in the middle of the clouds that they are tied to at around 40km. If they reach that mark they turn back and burn it to the bunker.

pockets.heading: Serpentis Base
lines: For missile spammers: I usualy start from killing BS in aggroing group, because its faster (Serpentis BS have a lot of defenders, from small distance they can't pop your missiles). Drones aggro.

For Tengus and other speedtanks: kill frigates from the first group, then the spies,then other ships (or when u are confident on your tank holding entire pocket (and don't want to switch ammo) kill all frigates first.

pockets.heading: First group, aggro
lines: (Web / if shot by second player or drones, they will trigger aggro from the entire pocket)

pockets.heading: Fourth, behind station, under group 3
lines: (Random Always spawn Random. Might drop faction loot. See the Serpentis/Gurista version for more info)

If you move towards the groups of ships or your drones move close to them you get aggro from them by proximity. Your drones will also draw aggro from every unaggressed ship in the pocket if they attack the Angel Spies, but enemies will deaggro upon recalling your drones to the drone bay.
non-ganged logistical ships seem to not get aggro. Up is a good direction to go.

pockets.heading: Blitz
lines: Edit: Gate is no longer unlocked, date of test September 5th 2012. Same applies to all other gates except the Warzone gates.
Old text: just kill the Guardian Veterans webbing you, then warp to The Research Outpost.

Alternative Blitz: warp in tanked decoy alt first, so you will not be webbed when warping in your main. This requires two Zbikoki's cards, if combined with the blitz for stage 1, but saves you another minute or so taking down the Guardian Veterans.

pockets.heading: War installation drops what looks like random junk T1 loot.
Gurista Base
lines: Guristas Spy (25k and 30k frigates) webbing, drones can aggro, same applies for group 4 - the Kyoukan will sometimes attack your drones.

pockets.heading: First group, aggro
lines: (Web / if shot by second player or drones, they will trigger aggro from the entire pocket)

pockets.heading: Both war stations drops what looks like random T1 junk loot.
Blitz
lines: None, gate is locked, kill all the ships in this stage to jump to the Research Outpost.
non-ganged logistical ships seem to not get aggro. Up is a good direction to go.
Salvagers Note: ( Items in Italics needs to be confirmed )
Reports of a random BS spawn are now no longer true - the BS which used to spawn chance based before is now renamed to "Supervisor" and always there at start.

Be advised when solo salvaging.  The ship is the Overseer.  It has about a 50% chance of physically being in the room in the beggining and another 50% chance of not coming in until a un-determined amount of time.

pockets.heading: First group (aggroing):
lines: Killing this group will unlock the Heron cargohold

pockets.heading: Third group:
lines: Gate in this room is now also locked, its not possible to warp to first room again until you clear all enemies (Date of test September 5th 2012).

pockets.heading: Mining
lines: In the Research Outpost are 32 Roids 703,056 Units Veldspar

pockets.heading: Structures
lines: Drug lab doesn't spawn additional ships, nor does the battlestation.
